page: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 16 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage, meaning storage must be attached externally.

The primary hardware feature of this server is its graphics processing configuration, which includes three NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture. These GPUs provide a combined total of 144 GB of VRAM. This dense GPU allocation makes the instance highly suitable for workloads requiring parallel processing capabilities, such as deep learning, large language model inference, and complex rendering tasks.

From a resource density perspective, the pairing of 192.0 GB of system memory with 144 GB of total GPU VRAM provides a balanced environment for handling large datasets in memory before feeding them to the accelerators. The shared CPU allocation suggests a qualitative cost-efficiency tradeoff, optimizing the instance for GPU-heavy tasks where continuous, dedicated physical CPU cores are less critical than raw GPU compute.
description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16 shared vCPUs, 192.0 GB of system memory, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, delivering a total of 144 GB of VRAM. This instance does not feature local storage, requiring external storage volumes. The combination of high system memory and substantial GPU VRAM makes this server highly efficient for memory-intensive parallel processing tasks. The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective resource profile for workloads that prioritize GPU compute over dedicated CPU cores. It is designed for workloads such as machine learning, large-scale data processing, and GPU-accelerated rendering.
